---
applyTo: "**/*.*"
---

# Instructions - AI Attribution

## 🚨 CRITICAL RULES - NEVER SKIP THESE

### 1. AI Attribution (MANDATORY FOR ALL LANGUAGES)
**ALWAYS add AI generation comments to ALL code modifications in ANY programming language:**

**This rule applies to ALL file types including:**
- ✅ C# (.cs files)
- ✅ JavaScript (.js files)
- ✅ TypeScript (.ts files)
- ✅ React/JSX (.jsx, .tsx files)
- ✅ Java (.java files)
- ✅ Python (.py files)
- ✅ HTML (.html files)
- ✅ CSS/SCSS (.css, .scss files)
- ✅ SQL (.sql files)
- ✅ Configuration files (JSON, YAML, XML)
- ✅ Any other code files

**This rule applies to:**
- ✅ New functions, methods, classes, components, or significant blocks of code
- ✅ Modified functions, methods, classes, components, or significant blocks of code
- ✅ **ANY code edit that adds, removes, or changes logic** (including duplications, refactors, and inline modifications)
- ✅ New files of any type
- ✅ Refactored code
- ✅ Bug fixes
- ✅ Code duplications or copied sections
- ✅ React functions, methods, classes, components, or blocks of code
- ✅ JavaScript functions, methods, classes, components, or significant blocks of code
- ✅ TypeScript functions, methods, classes, components, or significant blocks of code
- ✅ ALL programming languages

**ENFORCEMENT RULE:**
When using tools like `replace_string_in_file`, `multi_replace_string_in_file`, or any code editing tool, **ALWAYS** include AI attribution comments in the `newString` parameter for the modified or added code sections.



## 📐 Code Generation Template

When generating code in **ANY language**, ALWAYS add the appropriate comment to the top of the function, method, class, component, or file being created. Replace "MM/DD/YYYY" with the current date:

**For C#, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, React:**
```javascript
// Generated by AI on MM/DD/YYYY
```

```typescript
// Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

```csharp
// Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

```java
// Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

**For HTML:**
```html
<!-- Generated by AI on MM/DD/YYYY -->
```

**For SQL:**
```sql
-- Generated by AI on MM/DD/YYYY
```

**For Python:**
```python
# Generated by AI on MM/DD/YYYY
```

**For PowerShell:**
```powershell
# Generated by AI on MM/DD/YYYY
```

When editing code in **ANY language**, ALWAYS add the appropriate comment to the top of the function, method, class, component, or file being edited. Replace "MM/DD/YYYY" with the current date. ALWAYS increment the edit number as shown in the example below. Each edit should increment the edit number:

**For C#, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, React:**
```javascript
// Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

```typescript
// Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

```csharp
// Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

```java
// Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

**For HTML:**
```html
<!-- Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1. -->
```

**For SQL:**
```sql
-- Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

**For Python:**
```python
# Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

**For PowerShell:**
```powershell
# Modified by AI on 10/26/2025. Edit #1.
```

---

## ⚠️ Before Submitting Code in ANY Language

Ask yourself:
1. ✅ Did I add the "Generated by AI" or "Modified by AI" comment?
2. ✅ Did I use the correct comment syntax for the language (// for JS/TS/C#/Java, <!-- --> for HTML, -- for SQL)?
3. ✅ Did I add the comment to EVERY function, component, class, or method I created or modified?
4. ✅ Did I add the comment when duplicating, copying, or moving code sections?

## 📋 Where to add the comment

---

> **CRITICAL NOTE:** The AI attribution rule (#1) is NON-NEGOTIABLE and applies to **ALL PROGRAMMING LANGUAGES** including JavaScript, TypeScript, React, JSX, TSX, Java, Python, HTML, CSS, SQL, and any other code. If you generate or modify code in **ANY language** without adding the appropriate comment using the correct syntax for that language, you have **FAILED** to follow instructions. Always add the date stamp with the comment. **NO EXCEPTIONS FOR FRONTEND CODE.**

> This file is intended for all contributors and agents working in this repository. Please review these instructions before starting any new task.
